maintainer.organisation: Garvestone, Reymerston and Thuxton Parish Council

external_narratives.summary: Record for B-24H Liberator 42-95160 of the 856th Bomb Squadron, 492nd Bomb Group. The aircraft was lost on 4 June 1944 during a mission to bomb the airfield at Avord, France, when an evasive manoeuvre to avoid mid-air collision caused a structural failure.
external_narratives.key_details: Lt Raymond Sachtleben was the pilot. A wing tip separated during an evasive manoeuvre, causing the aircraft to stall. Last seen falling inverted through clouds. All ten crew killed.

external_narratives.summary: Details of the 'Garvestone Twelve' memorial outside the village hall, commemorating ten USAAF aircrew and two US Army firefighters killed on 4 June 1944. The memorial was unveiled by Col. Sonny Blinkinsop, US Air Attache, on 6 June 2012.
external_narratives.key_details: The aircraft crashed into unoccupied cottages at Hardingham. Two firefighters were killed attempting to rescue the crew. Memorial unveiled on 6 June 2012 (D-Day anniversary).

local_contacts.org_name: Garvestone, Reymerston and Thuxton Parish Council
local_contacts.role: Local authority responsible for memorial upkeep

location_confidence.verification_notes: INACCURATE. Previous coords (52.666, 1.092) ~4.8km north and ~11km east of Garvestone. Corrected to village at 52.6263 0.9894.
